quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Hungarian language has a special word, 'szívlelés,' which literally means 'to soul' something — describing the act of putting your heart and soul into what you do. It beautifully captures the Hungarian spirit of doing things with deep care and passion, whether it's cooking, gardening, or creating art.

short_read.title: The Secret Social Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals in the Strait of Juan de Fuca form close friendships and return to the same haul-out spots year after year. Scientists are discovering these playful marine mammals have far more complex social bonds than we ever imagined.

medium_read.title: The Healing Power of Garden Soil
medium_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that a harmless bacteria commonly found in garden soil can actually boost serotonin levels and improve mood — gardeners have known this secret all along! Learn why getting your hands dirty is genuinely good for your wellbeing.

long_read.title: The Danube: Europe's River of History and Culture
long_read.teaser: The beautiful Danube River flows through ten countries including Hungary, connecting centuries of history, music, and culture along its winding path. This journey along Europe's second-longest river reveals castles, composers, and the stories of millions who have called its banks home.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer in Summer
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot Columbian black-tailed deer around Port Angeles with their new fawns. These gentle creatures are most active during early morning and evening hours, and you might see them grazing near the edges of forests or even in some of the green spaces around town. Their reddish summer coats are noticeably lighter than their gray winter fur.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Yes! That tiny heart beats an astonishing 1,200 times per minute — what an incredible little bird!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute during flight — nearly 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Actually, a hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute — that's approximately 20 beats every single second!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To power their high-speed flight and rapid wing beats, hummingbirds have the fastest heart rate of any bird. At rest, their heart still beats 250 times per minute, and they must eat constantly to fuel their incredible metabolism.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'hello' or 'goodbye' depending on the situation?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Exactly right! 'Szia' is the friendly, informal way to say both hello and goodbye — such a versatile little word!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: It's 'szia' — this casual, warm word works for both greeting and parting, making it one of the most useful words in Hungarian!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Szia (pronounced 'SEE-ah') comes from the word 'szolga' meaning servant, as in 'your servant' — a humble greeting that evolved into today's friendly hello and goodbye. Its plural form, 'sziasztok,' is used when greeting multiple people.

puzzle_3.question: What percentage of the Earth's oxygen is produced by ocean phytoplankton?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Wonderful! About 50% of our oxygen comes from tiny ocean plants — the Strait of Juan de Fuca is part of this amazing system!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Actually, it's about 50%! These microscopic ocean plants produce half of the world's oxygen — every other breath you take comes from the sea.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Phytoplankton are microscopic plants that drift in the ocean, using photosynthesis just like land plants. The waters around Port Angeles are rich with these tiny organisms, which also form the base of the entire marine food chain.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
